completeness.notes: All 7 requirement categories covered. 12 functional requirements with clear acceptance criteria. 7 success criteria with baselines and targets. Comprehensive stakeholder analysis.

technical_soundness.notes: IBMi integration requirements well-documented. HIPAA constraints clearly captured. AI suitability correctly assessed as conditional_fit (score 3). Pain points quantified with cost estimates.

well_architected.notes: Security and compliance well-addressed. Performance NFRs specified. Scalability projections included. Sustainability not directly addressed at requirements level (appropriate — downstream concern).

clarity.notes: Clear problem statement with current/desired state. Scope boundaries explicit with justification. Assumptions documented. Stakeholder communication preferences specified.

feasibility.notes: Budget range ($500K-$1.5M) realistic for scope. 12-18 month timeline feasible with phased approach. Team constraint (5 internal IT) acknowledged with consulting plan. RPG developer retirement risk flagged.

overall.notes: Strong requirements document. Comprehensive discovery covering healthcare modernization complexity. HIPAA compliance requirements thoroughly captured.

findings.finding: No explicit data volume growth projections for DB2/400 — '10-15% annual growth' stated in NFRs but not broken down by module or data type.
findings.recommendation: Add per-module data growth estimates to inform capacity planning in architecture and estimate phases.

findings.finding: IE11 support constraint (Phase 1 only) may add 10-15% frontend development effort not explicitly quantified.
findings.recommendation: Quantify IE11 compatibility effort in estimate or remove constraint if client can upgrade workstations before Phase 1.

improvement_plan.action: Add data volume growth projections per module
improvement_plan.validation: Growth projections present for each of the 6 clinical modules

completeness.notes: 8 components, 6 data flows, 3 Mermaid diagrams, WA scores for all 6 pillars. Tech stack covers all layers. Alternatives considered with clear rejection rationale.

technical_soundness.notes: Strangler fig approach well-justified for healthcare risk profile. NestJS/React stack appropriate. IBMi DAL design addresses EBCDIC conversion, connection pooling, and circuit breaker patterns. Redis caching reduces IBMi load.

well_architected.notes: WA overall 7.3. Security pillar strong (8.5) given HIPAA focus. Reliability concern: IBMi as single point of failure acknowledged but mitigation limited to client-managed DR. Sustainability lowest (6.5) — acceptable for this engagement type.

clarity.notes: Executive summary clear with go/no-go recommendation. Mermaid diagrams effective for system context, deployment, and data flow. Component purposes well-documented.

feasibility.notes: Investment range ($750K-$1.2M) aligns with requirements budget. Node.js/IBM i Access integration has community precedent (node-jt400). Blue-green deployment on Fargate is proven pattern.

overall.notes: Solid architecture for healthcare IBMi modernization. Key strength: preserves proven RPG business logic while providing modern UI and IAM. Key risk: IBMi connection pool as bottleneck needs load testing.

findings.finding: IBMi connection pool limited to 50 concurrent connections. With 500+ users and 50,000 daily transactions, pool exhaustion under peak load is a realistic risk.
findings.recommendation: Conduct load testing with production-representative data volumes. Consider connection pool sizing based on peak concurrent queries, not users. Implement request queuing as backpressure mechanism.

findings.finding: Architecture specifies 'Keycloak or AWS Cognito' for IAM but doesn't resolve the choice. Different security postures and HIPAA compliance paths.
findings.recommendation: Resolve IdP choice in Foundation phase. Keycloak (self-hosted, full control) vs. Cognito (managed, less configuration). Both support HIPAA with BAA.

findings.finding: No offline capability design for mobile clinical workflows. FR-009 mentions 'offline capability for basic patient lookup' but architecture doesn't address it.
findings.recommendation: Add service worker / local storage pattern for critical offline scenarios (patient lookup, medication administration record). Design sync conflict resolution.

improvement_plan.action: Load test IBMi connection pool with production data volumes
improvement_plan.validation: Load test results showing pool behavior at 2x peak concurrent queries
IdP choice documented with HIPAA compliance evidence

gaps: Fargate vs EC2 cost crossover analysis needed

completeness.notes: Full STRIDE analysis with 8 threats. 13 HIPAA compliance entries. 5-layer defense-in-depth. 5 findings with remediation. IAM design with 7 roles.

technical_soundness.notes: STRIDE threats realistic for healthcare IBMi modernization. Mitigations are actionable and specific. HIPAA compliance mapping covers key controls. Defense-in-depth layers properly ordered.

well_architected.notes: Security pillar well-addressed. Compliance posture documented with specific control status (compliant/partial/non_compliant). Guardrails clearly stated.

clarity.notes: Threat descriptions clear with affected components traced to architecture. Compliance mapping actionable. Findings have clear recommendations.

feasibility.notes: Most security controls are achievable within timeline. Penetration testing finding (F-001) is critical but commonly deferred to pre-go-live. Contingency plan (F-004) may require additional budget.

overall.notes: Thorough HIPAA-focused security review. Strengths: comprehensive STRIDE analysis, practical mitigations. Key concern: penetration testing and contingency plan not budgeted.

findings.finding: Penetration testing (F-001 in security_review) flagged as critical but not included in project timeline or budget.
findings.recommendation: Add penetration testing to Phase 5 (Parallel Run) or Phase 6 (Cutover). Budget $15K-$25K for third-party pen test.

findings.finding: HIPAA contingency plan (F-004) marked as medium finding but HIPAA requires documented disaster recovery and emergency access procedures.
findings.recommendation: Elevate contingency plan to high priority. Document emergency access (break-the-glass) procedures, backup/recovery, and business continuity plan.

improvement_plan.validation: Pen test line item in estimate.json and project_plan.json Phase 5

completeness.notes: 7 phases covering full lifecycle including legacy assessment and parallel run. 8 milestones, 3 decision gates, 8 sprints, 7 risks, communication plan. Migration-specific section present.

technical_soundness.notes: Phase sequencing logical — legacy assessment before foundation, parallel run before cutover. Critical path identified. Dependencies mapped with internal and external items. Risk register covers technical, resource, and timeline risks.

well_architected.notes: Agile methodology with 2-week sprints. Decision gates at key junctures. Change management phased with the rollout. Communication plan addresses all stakeholder levels.

clarity.notes: Phase objectives and deliverables clearly stated. Milestone dates aligned with phase boundaries. Success criteria measurable. Sprint goals specific to phase objectives.

feasibility.notes: 68-week timeline fits the 12-18 month constraint. Phase overlaps (3 and 4, 5 and 6) are realistic parallel tracks. Risk: Clinical Modules Development (P-004, 24 weeks) is the longest phase and most likely to slip.

overall.notes: Solid project plan for healthcare modernization. Strengths: migration-specific phases (parallel run, cutover), decision gates. Key concern: P-004 duration and RPG developer availability.

findings.finding: Phase P-004 (Clinical Modules Development) spans 24 weeks with 6 modules. If any module encounters IBMi integration issues, the entire phase could slip, compressing parallel run time.
findings.recommendation: Consider splitting P-004 into sub-phases by module priority. Add a mid-phase checkpoint at week 28 (halfway through P-004) with go/no-go for remaining modules.

findings.finding: Sprint plan only covers 8 sprints (first ~16 weeks). Remaining 52 weeks of sprints not detailed.
findings.recommendation: Acceptable for planning depth — detailed sprints for near-term, high-level for future. Document that remaining sprints will be planned during execution using rolling wave planning.

findings.finding: External dependency on client IdP provisioning (needed_by 2026-05-18). If client delays IdP setup, Phase P-003 (IAM) is blocked.
findings.recommendation: Add fallback: use development IdP (e.g., Keycloak dev instance) for IAM development while client provisions production IdP. Document dependency escalation path.

improvement_plan.validation: Checkpoint milestone added at week 28 with scope adjustment authority

improvement_plan.action: Document rolling wave planning approach for future sprints
improvement_plan.validation: Rolling wave note in project_plan methodology section
